claim: Basic RAG fails ~10% of the time in retrieval; contextual retrieval reduces failure rate to <3%, improving RAG accuracy by up to 35%
actionable: Implement contextual embedding in RAG pipelines by prepending document context to each chunk; expect 35% improvement in retrieval success
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: When chunking documents for contextual retrieval, prepend a brief context summary (1-2 sentences) describing where the chunk fits within the document, separated from chunk content by triple-dash delimiters
actionable: For each document chunk, run a prompt with the full document and ask the LLM: 'Give a short succinct context to situate this chunk.' Prepend output as: [context] --- [chunk_content]
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: Prompt caching (OpenAI 50% cost reduction, Anthropic/Gemini ~90% reduction) makes contextual retrieval cost-effective even when sending full documents for every chunk
actionable: Use prompt caching when implementing contextual retrieval; enable it in API requests for providers that support it (OpenAI by default, Anthropic/Gemini via toggle)
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: Use cheap small models (GPT 4-1 Nano, Haiku) for contextual embedding generation since it requires minimal reasoning; reserve expensive models for retrieval and generation only
actionable: In RAG pipelines, route contextual embedding to a small cheap model (Nano/$0.03 per input 1M tokens) while using frontier models only for retrieval and final generation
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: Vision in RAG (raw screenshots) causes hallucination and confusion; simpler representations (text descriptions, structured data) are more reliable for LLM reasoning
actionable: Avoid embedding raw screenshot vision in RAG chunk context; instead provide structured text descriptions of entities and relationships
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: Contextual embeddings can be combined with hybrid search (BM25 keyword + semantic), re-ranking, and query expansion for further accuracy gains beyond the 35% baseline improvement
actionable: After implementing contextual embeddings, layer hybrid search and re-ranking; combine strategies rather than using any single approach alone
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: Use the same embedding model in both the RAG generation pipeline and in the retrieval agent; embedding model mismatch breaks semantic consistency
actionable: When implementing RAG, ensure the embedding model used to encode chunks during ingestion is identical to the embedding model used during retrieval queries
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG

claim: Neon (serverless Postgres) provides autoscaling and database branching, reducing operational overhead compared to fixed-instance Postgres platforms
actionable: For RAG systems using Postgres as vector store, use serverless options like Neon to avoid manual capacity management and enable feature-branch development workflows
source_title: The EASIEST Possible Strategy for Accurate RAG
